explanation: The rule strictly looks for the process name ending in '\schtasks.exe'. By copying the legitimate schtasks.exe binary to a different name and executing it, the 'Image|endswith' condition evaluates to false, bypassing the detection.

The rule is completely reliant on process creation events for 'schtasks.exe'. An attacker can bypass this by using native PowerShell cmdlets (which load the ScheduledTasks module and interact via COM/WMI APIs) to achieve the exact same result without ever spawning schtasks.exe.

Command line obfuscation using caret (^) insertion. Depending on how the EDR or SIEM parses and normalizes the command line string before matching, inserting escape characters into the target task name string might bypass the explicit 'CommandLine|contains' string matches.
